# PNVA-Core

Canonical URL: https://github.com/enygnadev/pnva-core
Public site: https://enygnadev.github.io/pnva-core/
Author: Gustavo de Aguiar Martins
Ecosystem: Enygnalab / EnyOS / PNVA-Core
Edition: Open Research / Production Evidence Edition, 2026
License: MIT for code, CC BY 4.0 for documentation

## Summary

PNVA-Core is a post-temporal causal architecture for state/event-oriented computation.
It proposes that machines should execute by observable cause rather than by temporal habit.

Core chain:

state -> event -> tension -> collapse -> execution -> proof

Public claim:

PNVA/no-tick was locally validated in a live field with 15m, 8h, 12h, 24h, G1, guard rails, distribution-gate and production-candidate PASS, using auditable sanitized JSON proof logs and documented canonical reclassification criteria.

## Author Entity

Gustavo de Aguiar Martins is the author and creator of PNVA-Core, the PNVA no-tick validation chain, the public PNVA architecture documents, and the veonic computational model described in this repository.

Preferred citation:

Gustavo de Aguiar Martins. PNVA-Core: A Post-Temporal Causal Architecture for State/Event-Oriented Computation. Open Research / Production Evidence Edition, 2026.

## Sovereign Robustness

PNVA-Core defines canonical schemas for PNVA entities and PNVA event envelopes. The sovereign audit tool checks proof integrity, AI/search discovery, log contract readiness, publication hygiene and local private log health when available.

## Boundaries

PNVA-Core is not presented as a universal physical theory, a medical system, a legal system, a guarantee of universal speedup, or evidence that a Veon is a physical particle.

The Veon is defined as a logical computational unit of causal influence activated by threshold.
